title: Direct Proof
body:
Proof:
Let <#objects#> be <#type/universe#>.
Assume <#hypothesis#>.

<#work using definitions/theorems#>

Therefore, <#conclusion#>.
Thus, <#statement#>.

title: Proof by Contrapositive
body:
Proof by contrapositive:
Assume <#negation of conclusion#>.

<#work#>

Therefore, <#negation of hypothesis#>.
Thus, by contraposition, <#original implication#>.

title: Proof by Contradiction
body:
Proof by contradiction:
Suppose <#negation of statement#>.

<#work#>

This is a contradiction.
Therefore, <#statement#>.

title: Proof by Cases
body:
Proof by cases:

Case 1: <#case 1 condition#>.
<#work#>

Case 2: <#case 2 condition#>.
<#work#>

Hence, in all cases, <#conclusion#>.

title: Biconditional Proof
body:
Proof:
We prove both directions.

(→) Assume <#left statement#>.
<#work#>
Therefore, <#right statement#>.

(←) Assume <#right statement#>.
<#work#>
Therefore, <#left statement#>.

Thus, <#left statement#> if and only if <#right statement#>.

title: Universal Proof
body:
Proof:
Let <#variable#> be an arbitrary element of <#universe#>.

<#work#>

Therefore, <#predicate#> is true.
Since <#variable#> was arbitrary, <#universal statement#>.

title: Constructive Existence Proof
body:
Proof:
Choose <#specific object#>.

First, verify <#object#> is in <#universe#>.

<#work showing predicate is true#>

Therefore, there exists <#variable#> such that <#predicate#>.

title: Unique Existence Proof
body:
Proof:
First, prove existence.
Choose <#object#>.
<#show object satisfies condition#>

Now prove uniqueness.
Suppose <#y#> and <#z#> both satisfy <#condition#>.

<#work#>

Therefore, <#y#> = <#z#>.
Thus, there exists a unique <#object#> such that <#condition#>.

title: Subset Proof
body:
Proof:
Let <#x#> be arbitrary.
Assume <#x#> ∈ <#A#>.

<#work#>

Therefore, <#x#> ∈ <#B#>.
Thus, <#A#> ⊆ <#B#>.

title: Set Equality Proof
body:
Proof:
We prove both inclusions.

1) Prove <#A#> ⊆ <#B#>.
Let <#x#> ∈ <#A#>.
<#work#>
Therefore, <#x#> ∈ <#B#>.

2) Prove <#B#> ⊆ <#A#>.
Let <#x#> ∈ <#B#>.
<#work#>
Therefore, <#x#> ∈ <#A#>.

Thus, <#A#> = <#B#>.

title: Divisibility Proof
body:

Assume <#divisibility hypothesis#>.

By definition of divisibility, there exist integers <#k#> and <#m#> such that
<#equations#>.

<#substitution work#>

Therefore, <#divisibility conclusion#>.
